name: pyquerystring

metadata.name: pyquerystring
metadata.license: Fancy query string parsing & application/x-www-form-urlencoded parsing
metadata.summary: Query String Parsing The Way It Should Be

Description: Python's default urlparse.parse_qs() does not understand the concept of data structures. While this works for simple querystrings, anything more complex returns a less than desirable result.
        
        This library will intelligently parse complex querystrings that python's library is unable to handle such as the following:
        
        mylist[]=item0&mylist[]=item1
        
        mylist[0]=item0&mylist[1]=item1
        
        mylist[0][0]=subitem0&mylist[0][1]=subitem1
        
        mylist.element=item0
        
        mylist.element[0]=item0&mylist.element[1]=item0
        
Platform: UNKNOWN

modules.name: pyquerystring.querystring
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: pyquerystring.querystring.QueryStringParser.process
documentation:
Given a key-value pair, assign the value to the location specified by
the key in the result attribute.

```pycon
>>> self.process('id[0]', 'foo')
self.result['id'][0] = 'foo'
```

methods.name: pyquerystring.querystring.QueryStringParser.parse
documentation: Break the key into tokens to determine where to assign the value

methods.name: pyquerystring.querystring.QueryStringParser.tokens
documentation: Returns an iterator of the array elements (tokens) of a given key
